text: The week after the Full Convergence. Strangely quiet meeting. Good quiet.

speaker: Vermia
text: I have a thing I want to say.

speaker: Vermia
text: I've been running this club for two years. I founded it because I needed it. I kept going because you all showed up. And this past month—

speaker: Vermia
text: I don't know if Scatha is real. I thought I knew. I was sure. Now I'm in a different kind of sure — not 'yes he's real' or 'no he's not'. More like — there's something real underneath things. Something that worms know about. And we can reach toward it with poems. And sometimes it reaches back a little.

speaker: Grubwin
text: That's the most theologically nuanced thing you've ever said.

speaker: Vermia
text: I've been thinking for a month.

speaker: Mawla
text: I think that's exactly right. That's what I believe too, I think. I just didn't have the words for it.

speaker: Dirtus
text: I still think Scatha is literally real and I'm preparing a follow-up convergence for the summer.

speaker: Dirtus
text: Thank you. I love all of you. Scatha loves all of you.

speaker: Grubwin
text: The things I've accepted as normal in my life.

speaker: Vermia
text: One more thing. The second hymn. I finished it. I want to sing it. Now. Together.

speaker: Mawla
text: I told you I'd help with the melody.

speaker: Vermia
text: I remembered. I wrote you a part.
And so they sing the second hymn. It's slower than the first. Less calling, more staying. You know all the words to the first one now; you learn the second one as it happens.

speaker: Vermia
text: 'We do not ask if you are coming / We do not ask if you were here / We press our ear against the turning / And call what we find worth — worth — worth—'
Mawla's harmony is low and steady. Dirtus's desk percussion is, for once, exactly right. Grubwin sings every word. You sing too.
